jokes.text: ARE YOU STONED? [LAUGHING] A LITTLE BIT.
jokes.setup: Nick's pseudo-philosophical rambling about heavy stuff and death
jokes.punchline: Lindsay cuts through with direct question, Nick's casual admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Lindsay's practical nature cutting through Nick's stoned philosophy. Economy of dialogue and natural delivery.

jokes.text: WHERE IS JOHN BONHAM RIGHT NOW? Like, is he in the ground rotting away somewhere, or maybe he's up in heaven... partying with Jimi and Janis.
jokes.setup: Stoned philosophical question about Led Zeppelin drummer
jokes.punchline: Morbid imagery followed by party heaven concept
jokes.explanation: Classic stoned teenager logic mixing morbid reality with wishful thinking. Good character integration for Nick.

jokes.text: THESE JOCKS THINK THEY'RE SUCH BADASSES, WALKING AROUND LIKE THEY CURED CANCER. THEY'RE JUST MONKEYS. [IMITATING MONKEY CHATTER]
jokes.setup: Kim's disdain for school spirit and jocks
jokes.punchline: Monkey comparison with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Kim's cynical attitude perfectly expressed. The monkey sounds add physical comedy element.

jokes.text: I THOUGHT YOU TWO WERE JOINED AT THE TONGUE OR SOMETHING.
jokes.setup: Daniel commenting on Lindsay and Nick's relationship
jokes.punchline: Crude twist on 'joined at the hip' expression
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's appropriately crude for Daniel's character. Good economy.

jokes.text: ARE YOU PREGNANT?
jokes.setup: Kim asking Lindsay for private conversation
jokes.punchline: Blunt assumption about why Lindsay wants to talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim bluntness. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: [CAVEMAN GRUNT] HELLO.
jokes.setup: Daniel arriving at conversation
jokes.punchline: Primitive grunt as greeting
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that fits Daniel's character as the dim burnout.

jokes.text: HE DIDN'T SEEM VERY PUMPED.
jokes.setup: Todd's underwhelming response to locker decoration
jokes.punchline: Cindy's matter-of-fact observation of his indifference
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan delivery of the obvious. Good character moment for Cindy's earnestness.

jokes.text: IT'S NOT, LIKE, A CRUSH. IT'S, LIKE, AN OBSESSION.
jokes.setup: Cindy correcting Sam about her feelings for Todd
jokes.punchline: Escalation from crush to obsession
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that reveals Cindy's intensity. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: THE MASCOT IS DEPRESSING ME. LOOK AT HIM. HE'S RUNNING IN PLACE. WHAT IS THAT? IS THAT SUPPOSED TO BE FUNNY?
jokes.setup: School pep rally with mascot performing
jokes.punchline: Neil's critical analysis of mascot performance
jokes.explanation: Neil's comedy nerd perspective critiquing physical comedy. Meta-humor about entertainment.

jokes.text: IT'S ALWAYS TODD SCHELLINGER.
jokes.setup: Todd being called up as team captain at assembly
jokes.punchline: Bill's weary observation of predictable favoritism
jokes.explanation: Perfect geek frustration with high school hierarchy. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: CHEERLEADERS HAVE TO DATE ATHLETES. IT'S THE LAW.
jokes.setup: Sam questioning why Cindy likes Todd
jokes.punchline: Bill's matter-of-fact explanation of social hierarchy as law
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about high school social rules delivered as literal law. High quotability.

jokes.text: SEE? HE CAN'T EVEN FALL FUNNY.
jokes.setup: Mascot falls during routine
jokes.punchline: Neil's professional comedy critique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neil line - comedy nerd critiquing physical comedy technique. Sets up his mascot ambitions.

jokes.text: MY COUSIN SLIPPED INTO A COMA ONCE. AND THEN WHEN HE WOKE UP, HE SPOKE FLUENT SPANISH.
jokes.setup: Discussing mascot's concussion risk
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about coma-induced language skills
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Bill moment. The matter-of-fact delivery sells the impossible claim perfectly.

jokes.text: I REALLY NEED A NEW MASCOT. I REALLY NEED SAM TO BE THE NEW MASCOT.
jokes.setup: Cindy saying they need a mascot
jokes.punchline: Bill's interpretation of subliminal message
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bill's overthinking and encouragement of Sam.

jokes.text: SHE WAS PRACTICALLY FEELING YOU UP.
jokes.setup: Bill analyzing Cindy's interaction with Sam
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation of innocent conversation
jokes.explanation: Teenage boy's exaggerated interpretation of normal interaction. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: BUT SAM'S NOT FUNNY.
jokes.setup: Competition for mascot position
jokes.punchline: Neil's blunt assessment of Sam
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neil bluntness. Economy of cruel honesty.

jokes.text: BEING THE SCHOOL MASCOT HAS ALWAYS BEEN MY DREAM.
jokes.setup: Neil trying to convince Sam to let him be mascot
jokes.punchline: Claiming lifelong mascot dreams
jokes.explanation: Absurd claim perfectly delivered with Neil's earnestness. Sets up the 'since lunch' callback.

jokes.text: YEAH, SINCE LUNCH.
jokes.setup: Neil's claim about lifelong mascot dreams
jokes.punchline: Sam revealing how recent this dream actually is
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with maximum economy. Deflates Neil's grandiose claim beautifully.

jokes.text: HELP ME, OBI-WAN KENOBI. YOU'RE MY ONLY HOPE.
jokes.setup: Sam asking Neil for help with mascot situation
jokes.punchline: Star Wars reference to manipulate Neil
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Sam understanding how to manipulate Neil through shared geek culture.

jokes.text: YOU HAD TO GO AND QUOTE THE STAR WARS, DIDN'T YOU?
jokes.setup: Sam's Star Wars reference
jokes.punchline: Neil recognizing he's being manipulated but can't resist
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Neil's self-awareness of his geek weaknesses.

jokes.text: I'M A MEMBER OF THE ROTARY, YOU KNOW. AND THE MASONS. AND COUNCILMAN APPLEBEE, JUST LAST WEEK, ASKED ME TO RUN FOR THE SCHOOL BOARD.
jokes.setup: Harold trying to establish his community importance
jokes.punchline: Listing increasingly minor civic accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold character moment - desperate for respect and status through minor civic involvement.

jokes.text: BOY, I COULD BE THE MAYOR, I WOULDN'T GET ANY RESPECT AROUND THIS HOUSE.
jokes.setup: Harold's litany of civic involvement ignored by family
jokes.punchline: Escalating to hypothetical mayor status
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Harold's need for validation. Classic dad comedy.

jokes.text: SLEEP TIGHT. DON'T LET THE BEDBUGS BITE.
jokes.setup: Nick climbing through Lindsay's window for romantic visit
jokes.punchline: Childish bedtime phrase as romantic goodbye
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick awkwardness - trying to be romantic but landing on childish phrase.

jokes.text: HEY, SAM, I HEARD THE MASCOT HAS TO CHANGE WITH THE CHEERLEADERS IN THE GIRLS' LOCKER ROOM.
jokes.setup: Bill giving Sam information about mascot duties
jokes.punchline: Obviously false claim designed to excite Sam
jokes.explanation: Standard teenage boy fantasy setup. Predictable but fits character dynamics.

jokes.text: REALLY? THAT DOESN'T SOUND RIGHT.
jokes.setup: Bill's locker room claim
jokes.punchline: Sam's understated skepticism
jokes.explanation: Good Sam reaction showing he's not completely naive but still hopeful.

jokes.text: SHE'S KINDA BOSSY.
jokes.setup: Cindy explaining her relationship with head cheerleader Vicki
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about Vicki's personality
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor. Cindy's niceness preventing her from being more critical.

jokes.text: FOR YOUR SAKE, I HOPE NOT.
jokes.setup: Sam asking about subliminal messages from Cindy
jokes.punchline: Bill's ominous warning about reading too much into it
jokes.explanation: Good Bill moment showing his protective friendship and realistic expectations.

jokes.text: YOU BETTER DITCH HIM. HE'S CRAZY.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking Heidi about dating Nick
jokes.punchline: Blunt warning with no elaboration initially
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Nick stalking revelation. Good economy and directness.

jokes.text: WELL, YOU KNOW, JUST BE CAREFUL WITH HER BECAUSE SHE'S A LIAR. SHE LIKES TO SCREW PEOPLE OVER.
jokes.setup: Lindsay talking to Heidi about Nick
jokes.punchline: Kim's warning about Heidi contradicting what we just heard
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows Heidi was telling truth but Kim paints different picture.

jokes.text: THAT GUY WAS AS FUNNY AS A CAR WRECK.
jokes.setup: Discussing the previous mascot's performance
jokes.punchline: Dark comparison to car accident
jokes.explanation: Dark Neil humor with immediate regret when he realizes the mascot is present.

jokes.text: IF YOU FEEL YOURSELF START TO FALL ASLEEP, PULL OUT A NOSE HAIR. IT'LL WAKE YOU RIGHT UP.
jokes.setup: Giving advice to concussed mascot about staying awake
jokes.punchline: Bizarre but practical solution to falling asleep
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd advice that sounds like it could actually work.

jokes.text: YOU COULD BE A DANCING YESHIVA STUDENT.
jokes.setup: Vicki suggesting Sam try out for the musical
jokes.punchline: Very specific cultural reference as casting suggestion
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific suggestion that's both encouraging and limiting.

jokes.text: IT'S ALL POLITICS!
jokes.setup: Sam being chosen as mascot over Neil
jokes.punchline: Neil's dramatic response to losing mascot position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neil dramatics - treating school mascot selection like corrupt government.

jokes.text: I KINDA THINK IT'S FUNNY WHEN THE JOCKS CRY, YOU KNOW? 'OOOOH, THEY CHEATED!' HA HA HA!
jokes.setup: Daniel explaining why he's going to the game
jokes.punchline: Sadistic enjoyment of athletic failure with mimicry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel cruelty masked as humor. The voice mimicry adds physical comedy.

jokes.text: [SOTTO] DON'T DO IT. PLEASE.
jokes.setup: Lindsay about to break up with Nick
jokes.punchline: Kim's desperate whispered plea
jokes.explanation: Great tension and character moment. Kim's genuine fear of Nick's reaction.

jokes.text: I'D HATE TO BE THAT GUY'S DRUM SET TONIGHT.
jokes.setup: Nick and Lindsay leaving together for potential breakup
jokes.punchline: Knowing Nick will take frustration out on drums
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that shows Daniel's understanding of Nick while being darkly funny. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: I WANTED TO BE FUNNY. VICKI DIDN'T WANT COMEDY.
jokes.setup: Sam struggling with mascot routine in practice
jokes.punchline: Understanding the mismatch between his goals and requirements
jokes.explanation: Good insight into the conflict between Sam's comedy aspirations and cheerleader expectations.

jokes.text: BE CAREFUL NOT TO PULL THEIR BRA STRAPS... 'CAUSE THEY'LL WIGGLE, AND YOU'LL FALL.
jokes.setup: Herbert giving Sam pyramid climbing advice
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate focus on intimate apparel for safety reasons
jokes.explanation: Teenage boy awkwardness masked as helpful advice. Appropriately cringey.

jokes.text: THAT BETTER HAVE BEEN WATER. THAT'S ALL I'M SAYIN'.
jokes.setup: Getting hit with water balloon from rival school
jokes.punchline: Suspicious about the liquid's actual contents
jokes.explanation: Kim's paranoid but reasonable concern given high school prank escalation.

jokes.text: I THINK HE'D BE CUTE WITH THAT MASK ON AND EVERYTHING. IT WOULD BE SO FUNNY.
jokes.setup: Jean watching Sam prepare for mascot debut
jokes.punchline: Maternal pride mixed with anticipation of comedy
jokes.explanation: Sweet mom moment showing Jean's supportive but slightly oblivious nature.

jokes.text: [WHISPERS] I'm not here.
jokes.setup: Nick calling while Lindsay hides from relationship
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's whispered instruction to her mother
jokes.explanation: Classic avoidance comedy. Good physical staging implied.

jokes.text: SHE'S, UM... UH, OUT WITH HER FATHER. YEAH. I DON'T KNOW WHAT THEY'RE DOING, BUT THEY'RE OUT THERE DOING IT.
jokes.setup: Jean forced to lie to Nick about Lindsay's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Awkward improvisation that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Jean's discomfort with lying creates awkward over-explanation. Good character moment.

jokes.text: LINDSAY, I DON'T LIKE TO LIE.
jokes.setup: Jean having just lied unconvincingly to Nick
jokes.punchline: Stating her moral discomfort immediately after lying badly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean character moment - moral but helpful, with immediate regret.

jokes.text: ARE YOU GONNA ANSWER ME, KEENNN?
jokes.setup: Nick answering phone expecting Ken but getting silence
jokes.punchline: Elongated, desperate plea to empty phone line
jokes.explanation: Great Nick vulnerability and neediness. The elongated name delivery is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: FINE. IF THIS ISN'T KEN, YOU BETTER NOT CALL BACK HERE... OR I'LL FIND YOU.
jokes.setup: Nick's earlier paranoid phone message
jokes.punchline: Escalation to actual threats against unknown callers
jokes.explanation: Escalates Nick's instability to genuinely threatening. The comedy comes from the absurdity of threatening random callers.

jokes.text: I FEEL LIKE... A MOTHER, YOU KNOW?
jokes.setup: Jean successfully giving Lindsay relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Realizing she's finally connecting with her daughter maternally
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment of parental triumph. Jean's wonder at successful mothering is endearing.

jokes.text: YOU'RE ON MY LEG.
jokes.setup: Jean getting physically affectionate in bed
jokes.punchline: Harold's practical interruption of romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold practicality deflating romantic moment. Good physical comedy implied.

jokes.text: WHY DON'T YOU TELL HIM HE'S A GREASEBUCKET, AND YOU DESERVE BETTER?
jokes.setup: Harold offering Lindsay breakup advice
jokes.punchline: Blunt, old-fashioned insult as relationship counsel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold moment - outdated terminology and direct approach to complex teen relationship.

jokes.text: I GOT TO FORM A HUMAN PYRAMID ON TOP OF HER.
jokes.setup: Sam explaining why Cindy might like him
jokes.punchline: Innocent physical contact interpreted as romantic connection
jokes.explanation: Great teenage boy logic - interpreting mandatory athletic activity as romantic progress.

jokes.text: WELL, THAT'LL DO IT.
jokes.setup: Sam's explanation of pyramid formation with Cindy
jokes.punchline: Harold's dry acknowledgment of teenage romantic logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect dad response - understanding teenage boy logic while being slightly amused by it.

jokes.text: MAYBE HE JUST GETS SO HORNY, HIS BRAIN FRIES, YOU KNOW?
jokes.setup: Discussing Nick's behavior with girlfriends
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate psychological analysis
jokes.explanation: Daniel's crude but perceptive analysis of teenage male psychology.

jokes.text: OH, IS THAT WHY YOU'RE SUCH A BASTARD TO ME?
jokes.setup: Daniel's advice about not showing too much interest in girls
jokes.punchline: Kim connecting the advice to Daniel's treatment of her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that recontextualizes Daniel's behavior as deliberate romantic strategy.

jokes.text: YEAH. WELL, YOU'RE STILL HERE, AREN'T YA?
jokes.setup: Kim calling out Daniel's relationship strategy
jokes.punchline: Daniel confirming the strategy works
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline that confirms the twisted logic works. Great relationship dynamic revelation.

jokes.text: A MILLION THINGS. YOU FALL ON IT. YOU STAB YOURSELF. YOU STAB THE OTHER MASCOT.
jokes.setup: Neil explaining what to do with a prop sword
jokes.punchline: Escalating from self-harm to attacking others
jokes.explanation: Classic Neil escalation from slapstick to violence as comedy. Shows his comedy theory knowledge.

jokes.text: A NORSEMAN IN HAWAII. THAT'S FUNNY!
jokes.setup: Neil demonstrating hula dance in Viking costume
jokes.punchline: Explaining why cultural mashup is comedic
jokes.explanation: Neil's comedy theory in action - explaining why incongruity creates humor.

jokes.text: I CAN'T BE SEEN WITH YOU.
jokes.setup: Neil doing embarrassing comedy routine in public
jokes.punchline: Bill's social self-preservation
jokes.explanation: Good Bill moment showing even geeks have social awareness limits.

jokes.text: TELL MY MOTHER THAT I LOVE HER.
jokes.setup: Confrontation with Lincoln students escalating
jokes.punchline: Daniel's dramatic farewell before potential fight
jokes.explanation: Classic coward's dramatic farewell. Daniel's fear masked as noble sacrifice.

jokes.text: WHO THE HELL ARE YOU?
jokes.setup: Neal in mascot costume after Sam abandons it
jokes.punchline: Vicki's confusion at identity switch
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Vicki's angry confusion at the switch.

jokes.text: TODD'S A JERK. HE'S NOT EVEN NICE TO YOU. HE'S ALL COCKY. IT'S JUST STUPID THAT YOU LIKE HIM.
jokes.setup: Cindy excitedly telling Sam about Todd asking her out
jokes.punchline: Sam's jealous outburst disguised as concern
jokes.explanation: Great Sam moment showing teenage jealousy poorly disguised as protective concern.

jokes.text: YOU THINK STEVE MARTIN ALWAYS FEELS LIKE BEING FUNNY? NO, BUT HE GOES OUT ON THAT STAGE EVERY NIGHT WITH HIS ARROW AND HIS BANJO, AND HE KILLS.
jokes.setup: Sam not wanting to perform as mascot
jokes.punchline: Neil's professional comedy pep talk with specific references
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neil comedy nerd moment with specific Steve Martin references. Shows his deep comedy knowledge.

jokes.text: TEENAGERS. WHAT ARE YOU GONNA DO?
jokes.setup: Harold's friends meeting Lindsay's disheveled burnout friends
jokes.punchline: Harold's diplomatic dismissal of embarrassing situation
jokes.explanation: Classic parent damage control line. Harold's attempt to save face with community leaders.

jokes.text: MOM, DID YOU BREAK UP WITH MY BOYFRIEND?
jokes.setup: Lindsay realizing Jean accidentally ended her relationship
jokes.punchline: Accusatory question about unintentional breakup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant culmination of Jean's well-intentioned meddling. Perfect economy and escalation.

jokes.text: MAN WITH A BIG HEAD COMING THROUGH.
jokes.setup: Neil in oversized mascot head navigating crowd
jokes.punchline: Literal description that sounds like ego reference
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that works on multiple levels - physical reality and ego metaphor.

jokes.text: OH, I STABBED MYSELF! OH, MY GOD. HE'S GONNA DIE. HELP ME. I'M DYING.
jokes.setup: Neil using prop sword during game
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic reaction to fake sword
jokes.explanation: Callback to Neil's earlier sword comedy theories put into practice with typical overacting.
